methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Occam is a seed-stage biotech and health company founded by Ana Montero Horas and Andre Marques-Smith. The company has raised $14M to date. The valuation universe comprises 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Tempus AI, Abridge, Ultima Genomics, Eikon Therapeutics, and Freenome.

Of three valuation methods evaluated, only stage-progression analysis proved applicable. The peer-median approach was not viable due to insufficient narrow-filter matches within the Biotech & Health sector—while 16 same-sector peers exist, fewer than 3 met strict same-stage or adjacent-stage and similar-tier criteria. Public-comp anchoring could not be executed because Occam's revenue is not disclosed, which is typical for private companies. The stage-progression method applies a 2.5x multiplier to the $14M raised as a proxy base value, yielding $35M.

Given reliance on a single method, the valuation range is $30M to $40M, with a median of $35M. This represents modest confidence: the range reflects typical seed-to-next-stage progression rather than corroborating multiple independent approaches. Key uncertainties include the reliability of using total raised as a valuation proxy for an early-stage company and lack of visibility into revenue trajectory or capital efficiency metrics that would support public-comp or revenue-based approaches. Additional disclosure or peer-set expansion would materially strengthen conviction.
